item.description:
An area is a place where things can be. A room, a field, a street corner, a clearing in a forest, a ship’s deck, a webpage — all areas. TAB defines an area as “a container with exits.” That’s the whole idea: an area holds things, and it connects to other areas through exits.

An area has contents — the things currently in it. A kitchen contains a table, chairs, dishes, a person cooking. A park contains trees, benches, people, squirrels. The contents change as things arrive and leave. The area persists.

A container is anything that holds something else inside it. A box is a container. A bag is a container. A room, a drawer, a pocket, a folder, a cell, a shipping container, a zip file — all containers.

The defining feature is an inside and an outside with a boundary between them. The boundary can be rigid (a box), flexible (a bag), permeable (a net), virtual (a directory on a filesystem), or conceptual (a category). What matters is that the boundary creates a distinction between what is in and what is not in.

A person is a thing that can act, move, speak, and be held responsible. People are the things that do things on purpose. A rock sits where it is. A person walks somewhere because they want to go there.

A person is an object — they have mass, take up space, exist physically. A person is mobile — they move between areas under their own power. But a person is more than a mobile object. A person has agency: they choose, they communicate, they make promises, they break them. A cart is mobile but not a person. A robot that moves is mobile but arguable. A sleeping human is still a person — agency is a capacity, not a continuous activity.

A vessel is a hollow object made to hold something. A cup is a vessel. A jar is a vessel. A bottle, a bowl, a barrel, a tank, a ship’s hull — all vessels.

A vessel has an inside separated from an outside by a wall. The wall gives the inside its shape. A cup is open at the top. A bottle narrows to a neck. A barrel is closed all around except for a bung. The shape of the inside determines what the vessel can hold and how you get things in and out.

An endeavor is an organized, sustained, failure-open striving toward an aim, commissioned by the striver. It has five parts:

  1. Committing agent — the person or unified group who takes up the endeavor. Not a participant but the locus of commitment. Without a determinate committer, there is activity but not endeavor.
  2. Directed aim — the orientation toward which effort is directed. Specific enough to distinguish striving from drifting, but the aim need not be fully specified in advance. You can endeavor to “understand physics” — an aim with no crisp terminal deliverable.
  3. Self-commitment — the agent’s placement of themselves in quasi-positional obligation toward the aim. Self-generated, no external sender required. This is what distinguishes endeavor from mandate or duty.
  4. Sustained exertion — the temporal structure: not a single act but a structure unfolding across multiple episodes, attempts, setbacks, and adjustments. A single try is an attempt. Sustained committed trying is an endeavor.
  5. Failure-openness — the aim is not guaranteed by the endeavoring. The endeavor can succeed, fail, or be abandoned. Without this, the activity is a procedure, not an endeavor.

Five properties make something an endeavor:

An object is a thing that exists as physical matter. It has mass, takes up space, and can be observed. A rock, a cup, a chair, a person’s body, a planet — all objects.

Objects have two properties that non-physical things lack: they are massive (they have weight and resist being moved) and they are notable (they can be seen, touched, or otherwise sensed). A thought has neither property. A number has neither. An object has both.

A thing is anything that can be distinguished from everything else. If you can point at it, name it, or talk about it, it is a thing. A rock is a thing. A color is a thing. A number, a feeling, a country, a mistake — all things. “Thing” is the most general word there is. Everything in this library is a thing.

A thing has qualities — properties that make it distinguishable. A red ball is distinguished from a blue ball by color. A heavy box is distinguished from a light box by weight. Without at least one quality, there is nothing to distinguish, and so nothing to call a thing.

item.title: Act
item.description: An act is (definition:: a manifestation of a thing’s energy resulting in change to it or its environment).

item.title: Agenda
item.description: An agenda is a collection of all tasks, whether they have a deadline or not.

item.title: Ai Assistant
item.description: An AI assistant is a [Large Language Model](Large Language Model) set up to provide a [conversational information interface](conversational information interface) that provides generalized help to its user|users.

item.title: Ai Conversation Transcript
item.description:
An AI conversation transcript is (definition:: a transcript of a conversation with an AI).

table topic
where type = [AI conversation transcript](AI conversation transcript)

item.title: Argumentative Essay
item.description:
An argumentative essay is (definition:: an essay for arguing a truth about a thing).

There is an [Argumentative Essay Catalog](Argumentative Essay Catalog).

item.title: Atom
item.description: An atom is (definition:: a particle composed of electron|electrons, proton|protons, and neutron|neutrons, and is the [basic unit](basic unit) of chemistry).

item.title: Bedtime Story
item.description: A bedtime story is (definition:: a story to help someone with [falling asleep](falling asleep)).

item.title: Buffalo Prarie
item.description: A buffalo prairie is (definition:: a prairie that has buffalo as a [keystone species](keystone species)).

item.title: Castille Soap
item.description: Castille soap is (definition:: a soap made from [olive oil](olive oil) and [sodium hydroxide](sodium hydroxide)).

item.title: Community Garden
item.description:
A community garden is an area of land used for horticulture by more than one person, in either an organizing|organized or improvising|improvised way, either individualizing|individually or collectivising|collectively.

Around the [United States](United States), most gardens are organized around the allocation of plots within the garden to individuals, who are responsible for its cultivating|cultivation and harvesting|harvest, if applicable. Some, and most elsewhere in the world, are more collective: gardeners share the entire garden and plots, if used, are designed around function, not property.

item.title: Condition
item.description: A condition is a quality|quality of the state of a thing at a given time. That is, at any moment, a thing is in a state, defined by its qualities, and the state of any one of those qualities in that moment is a condition of that thing.

item.title: Content Catalog
item.description:
A content catalog is (definition:: a [catalog of content](catalog of content)).

Perhaps confusingly, there’s a [Content Catalog Catalog](Content Catalog Catalog).

item.title: Conversational Information Interface
item.description: A conversational information interface may be defined as a type of [user interface](user interface) designed to [simulate conversation|emulate conversation](simulate conversation|emulate conversation) with a human being. It enables users to interact with software through [natural language dialogue](natural language dialogue), leveraging advanced artificial intelligence algorithms to understand and respond to user input. This interface facilitates intuitive and efficient [information retrieval](information retrieval), notetaking, and aids in various cognitive processes including brainstorming and organizing thoughts. It enhances productivity by allowing users to generate content at a faster rate than with traditional typing methods.

item.title: Daily Debrief
item.description: list where type = "[daily debrief](daily debrief)"

item.title: Digital Computer
item.description: A digital computer is a tool for managing information and connections. It translates our thought|thoughts and command|commands into the vast [digital universe](digital universe). Through this relationship, we expand our capability|capabilities to communicating|communicate, learning|learn, create [virtual environment|virtual environments](virtual environment|virtual environments) like [Synthetic Place of Ritualized Ethnosis|SPOREs](Synthetic Place of Ritualized Ethnosis|SPOREs), and ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating|relate more fully with both the [material universe](material universe) around us and the intangible [conceptual universe|realms of ideas](conceptual universe|realms of ideas).

item.title: Digital Garden
item.description: A digital garden is a term used to describe a [personal website](personal website) or [online platform](online platform) that serves as a collection of thoughts, ideas, and knowledge in a non-linear and evolving manner. It is a metaphorical representation of a real garden, where ideas and information are cultivated, nurtured, and interconnected. Unlike traditional blogs or websites, digital gardens are not organized chronologically or in a hierarchical structure. Instead, they focus on creating a network of interconnected ideas through hyperlinks, allowing visitors to explore and discover information in a non-linear way. Digital gardens often encourage collaboration, feedback, and continuous improvement, as they are designed to be living and evolving spaces for knowledge sharing.

item.title: Disney
item.description: Disney is a [media conglomerate](media conglomerate) that uses settler-colonialism to [genociding culture|genocide culture](genociding culture|genocide culture) through its [cultural appropriating|appropriation](cultural appropriating|appropriation) and [cultural misrepresenting|misrepresentation](cultural misrepresenting|misrepresentation) of humanity’s culture and story|stories.

item.title: Document
item.description: A document is pretty much any type of text-heavy file that isn’t some other type of thing like an essay or story.

item.title: Embodied Vernacular Autoethnosis
item.description: Embodied vernacular autoethnosis is (definition:: any artifact or [material change](material change) that came through [vernacular autoethnosis](vernacular autoethnosis)). Examples of embodied vernacular autoethnosis are the (example:: [enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone](enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone)), our (example:: [Groundhog Begleri|begleri](Groundhog Begleri|begleri)), or the (example:: water vapor rising from the [202 Soilworks](202 Soilworks)).

item.title: Emsenn.Net
item.description:
emsenn.net is the [top-level Internet domain](top-level Internet domain) I’ve used for my [personal website](personal website). As of 2023-12-24, it is used to host [Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my Webplace](Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my Webplace).

During [Arab Spring](Arab Spring), emsenn.net was used for syndicating the writing of anarchist|anarchists in the [Middle East and North Africa](Middle East and North Africa) region, specializing in Kurd|Kurdish perspectives.

item.title: Energetic Herbalism
item.description:
Energetic herbalism is (definition:: a [herbalism practice](herbalism practice) that is based on a truth that the essence of nature and human are the same.)

It is defined in [Energetic Herbalism, A Guide to Sacred Plant Traditions Integrating Elements of Vitalism, Ayurveda, and Chinese Medicine](Energetic Herbalism, A Guide to Sacred Plant Traditions Integrating Elements of Vitalism, Ayurveda, and Chinese Medicine), by [Kat Maier](Kat Maier).

item.title: Energy
item.description: The force that enables a thing to act.

item.title: Environment
item.description: An environment is things around a thing; what is around it in space at that time; its relationship|relationships.

item.title: Essay
item.description:
• (act:: editing|edit) the essay definition to outsource the definitions of essay subtypes [created:: 2023-12-22 ]

An essay is (definition:: an artifact that results from writing, usually a few hundred to several thousand word|words, usually focused on one or just a few topic|topics.)

Here’s a listing of various types of essays:

table definition
where file.name = "analytical essay" OR
  file.name = "argumentative essay" OR
  file.name = "comparative essay" OR
  file.name = "critical essay" OR
  file.name = "descriptive essay" OR
  file.name = "expository essay" OR
  file.name = "narrative essay" OR
  file.name = "reflective essay"
  • [expository essay|Expository essays](expository essay|Expository essays) are for informing people about a concept or topic.
  • [descriptive essay|Descriptive essays](descriptive essay|Descriptive essays) are for describing a thing, often focusing on one quality or set of qualities.
  • [narrative essay|Narrative essays](narrative essay|Narrative essays) are for [narrative storytelling](narrative storytelling).
  • [argumentative essay|Argumentative essays](argumentative essay|Argumentative essays) are for arguing a truth about a thing.
  • [Analytical essay|Analytical essays](Analytical essay|Analytical essays) are for ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating a concept or topic to event|events or information.
  • [comparative essay|Comparative essays](comparative essay|Comparative essays) are for looking at what is similar and different between two or more thing|things.
  • [critical essay|Critical essays](critical essay|Critical essays) apply a concept or synthesis of concepts to events or information.
  • [reflective essay|Reflective essays](reflective essay|Reflective essays) explore my relationship to a thing.

Check the [Essay Catalog](Essay Catalog) if you want to see all the essays that are a part of [Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my virtual mycelium](Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my virtual mycelium).

item.title: Etymology
item.description:
Ontogenrism is a term used in contemporary critical theory to describe a mode of world-formation in which reality is structured according to pre-existing narrative, categorical, or generic forms. It refers to the process by which beings, events, and relationships are made intelligible—and governable—by being shaped to fit into recognizable genres of existence. These genres may be social, emotional, political, linguistic, or ontological.

Ontogenrism is not limited to literary or cultural genre, but extends to broader epistemic and affective structures. It describes how the form of knowing, feeling, and perceiving is preformatted by expectations that precede experience. As such, ontogenrism operates as both a mechanism of recognition and a constraint on what can be known, imagined, or related to.

item.title: Etymology
item.description:
The deliberate practice of a person or people shaping their own culture through the creation and development of ritual|rituals, story|stories, [language pattern|language patterns](language pattern|language patterns), and symbol|symbols built from their [self-determined beliefs and values](self-determined beliefs and values). This concept is deeply rooted in the intentional pursuit of self-determination and cultural autonomy within communities that seek to resist dominant or oppressive systems.

Vernacular ethnosis is an emergent property of social interactions that occur at the grassroots level within a community or culture. It involves the organic development of shared understandings and values that are expressed through common speech, rituals, art forms, and other cultural expressions unique to a group’s lived experience.

item.title: Everything
item.description: Everything is every thing, taken together without separation based on quality.

item.title: Fair Dinkums
item.description: A way of acknowledging that you were wrong and the other right, after appreciating some information they brought in that you weren’t thinking about.

item.title: From Wikipedia
item.description:
Land is are the solid parts of the Earth we receive our bodies from and live our lives on.

From Wikipedia

land, also known as dry land, ground, or earth, is the solid terrestrial surface of Earth not submerged by the ocean or another body of water. It makes up 29.2% of Earth’s surface and includes all continents and islands. Earth’s land surface is almost entirely covered by regolith, a layer of rock, soil, and minerals that forms the outer part of the crust. Land plays important roles in Earth’s climate system, being involved in the carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, and water cycle. One third of land is covered in trees, another third is used for agriculture, and one tenth is covered in permanent snow and glaciers. The remainder consists of desert, savannah, and prairie.

item.title: Gaz Autoethnographic Project
item.description: The GAZ Autoethnographic Project is (function:: autoethnography about the [Groundhog Autonomous Zone](Groundhog Autonomous Zone)) being done with [Hannah Kass](Hannah Kass).

item.title: Groundhog Begleri
item.description: Groundhog Begleri are (definition:: begleri made through the [enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone](enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone)).

item.title: Hydrogen Atom
item.description: A hydrogen atom is (definition:: an atom of hydrogen, consisting of (typically) one proton and one electron).

item.title: Hydrogen Ion
item.description: A hydrogen ion is (definition:: a [hydrogen atom](hydrogen atom) which lacks an electron; in other words, a hydrogen ion is a single [hydrogen proton](hydrogen proton).)

item.title: Information
item.description: information is an abstract concept that refers to something which has the power to inform. At the most fundamental level, it pertains to the interpretation (perhaps formally) of that which may be sensed, or their abstractions. Any natural process that is not completely random and any observable pattern in any medium can be said to convey some amount of information. Whereas digital signals and other data use discrete signs to convey information, other phenomena and artifacts such as analogue signals, poems, pictures, music or other sounds, and currents convey information in a more continuous form. Information is not knowledge itself, but the meaning that may be derived from a representation through interpretation.

item.title: Knowledge
item.description: Knowledge is the accumulated information and understanding about a thing or set of things.

item.title: Log Entry
item.description:
A log entry is an item in a log, a log being a record consisting of a timestamp and information about the way some thing was at that time.

One type of log entry is a [personal log entry](personal log entry).

item.title: Managing
item.description: Managing is (definition:: the act of organizing…)

item.title: Mass
item.description:
Mass is a fundamental property of object|objects, quantification|quantifying the amount of matter they contain.

It is a measuring|measure of an object’s resistance to acceleration when a force is applied, often referred to as inertia. Mass also determines the strength of an object’s [gravity|gravitational attraction](gravity|gravitational attraction) to other objects with mass. Unlike weight, which can vary depending on gravity, mass is constant regardless position in space. In ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-02/strata/physics, it is typically measured in grams and plays a crucial role in [Newtonian mechanics](Newtonian mechanics) and [Einstein’s theory of relativity](Einstein’s theory of relativity).

item.title: Material Universe
item.description:
The material universe is (definition:: the collective term for all [matter|physical matter](matter|physical matter), energy, and space that is ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating together).

It includes celestial bodies like star|stars, planet|planets, galaxy|galaxies; phenomenon|phenomena such as gravity, light, and time; and the [fundamental particles](fundamental particles) that make up atom|atoms. This vast expanse relates through ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-02/strata/physics and encompasses everything from the largest galactic structures to the smallest subatomic particles. The material universe is where life takes form, [civilization collapse|civilizations collapse](civilization collapse|civilizations collapse), and where you probably are as you’re reading this.

item.title: Matter
item.description:
Matter is the substance of object|objects: thing|things with mass and volume.

It includes solid|solids, liquid|liquids, gas|gases, and plasma: states that are perceptible to our [human senses|senses](human senses|senses) or detectable with tool|tools. Composed of atom|atoms and molecule|molecules bound by force|forces at various scales, physical matter forms the building blocks of planet|planets, organism|organisms, and everyday objects. It is characterized by occupying space and responding to fundamental forces such as gravity and electromagnetism. Physical matter is a key component of the [material universe](material universe) we ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating|relate with through life.

item.title: Obsidian
item.description: Obsidian is (definition:: a [personal knowledge management software](personal knowledge management software)) and what I use for writing and managing [Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my Webplace](Groundhog Autonomous Zone|my Webplace).

item.title: Obsidian Task
item.description:
An Obsidian task is (definition:: a way of recording a task in Obsidian that can be used by the Dataview and [Obsidian Tasks](Obsidian Tasks) plugins). There is an online user guide.

In the [enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone|GAZ](enc/def/Groundhog Autonomous Zone|GAZ), we use the Dataview format for our tasks:

item.title: Punk Ash
item.description: https://www.crudemag.com/punk-ash

item.title: Pursuit Of Total Liberation
item.description: The pursuit of total liberation is (definition:: a ../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-02/strata/term for the practice of anarcho-nihilism).

item.title: Quality
item.description: A quality is any component of a thing which can be observing|observed or measuring|measured, including both [emergent qualities](emergent qualities) and attributes.

item.title: Radio Resistance
item.description:
Radio Resistance is (definition:: a [online radio station](online radio station) focused on sharing useful and interesting information and music between [radical person|radicals](radical person|radicals)). Listen here. Led by @arrrg@kolektiva.social.

  • (act:: writing|Write) a statement for [Radio Resistance](Radio Resistance) [created:: 2023-12-28 ]

item.title: Reading Note
item.description:
A reading note is a note taken about something I’m reading.

There’s a [Reading Note Catalog](Reading Note Catalog)

item.title: Soap
item.description: Soap is (definition:: a combination of an alkali (usually [sodium hydroxide](sodium hydroxide)) and [fatty acid](fatty acid), which is used as a surfactant and emulsifier, for cleaning).

item.title: Social Contribution Score
item.description:
This (type:: definition) exists within the (fiction:: [Durham 2030](Durham 2030)) fiction.

A social contribution score (SCS) is (definition:: a metric used to measure an individual’s contribution to their arcology). Score is related to work productivity, adherence to societal rules, or other behaviors deemed beneficial by the governing systems or authorities. The score influences a person’s access to resources, with higher scores granting access to additional goods, like synthetic protein bars for those above level 40. Conversely, lower scores may result in negative consequences, such as the possibility of being sent to forced labor camps indicated for those with scores significantly lower than Jaxson’s 35. This system is centrally monitored and regulated by an entity or system referred to as “Seldon,” with scores subject to periodic review. The Social Contribution Score is a tool of social control, impacting every aspect of an individual’s life within the arcology, including their livelihood and social privileges.

item.title: State
item.description: A state is (definition:: the composite of all condition|conditions, or specific quality|qualities, that define a thing at any given moment in time)

item.title: Strategy Document
item.description: A strategy document is a document for planning a strategy.

item.title: Synthetic Place Of Ritualized Ethnosis
item.description: Both the starting point and whole of a specific type of [virtual environment](virtual environment) for an individual to engage in [vernacular autoethnosis](vernacular autoethnosis). SPOREs are closely related to [digital garden|digital gardens](digital garden|digital gardens), but are specifically oriented toward [constructivism|constructivist learning practies](constructivism|constructivist learning practies).

item.title: Task
item.description: A task is one or more act|acts that have been associated with a goal. Tasks have an implicit or explicit procedure, that instruct one how to do the task. Tasks whose procedure (implicitly or explicitly) encodes the meaning of the task may be a ritual.

item.title: Tool
item.description:
A tool is an object that extends the capability|capabilities of a thing.

It can be as simple as a hammer aiding in driving nails or as complex as a [digital computer](digital computer) managing vast amounts of information. Tools are [../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating|relational extensions](../../../../slop/relationality/ai-generated-relationality/relationality-slop-2026-03-03/terms/relating|relational extensions) of our intending|intentions; they carry out tasks that amplify our efforts, facilitate new possibilities, and enhance our ability to shape and understand the [cosmos|world around us](cosmos|world around us).

item.title: Triggers
item.description:
If youre relating to triggers as events and not the whole as triggering and it being a matter of how you relate to those triggers not avoiding them

Triggers aren’t events, they’re phenomenon that emerge from what has happened in an environment, so if you relate to triggers just as events happening then you don’t see their source. if you don’t relate to them at the source they won’t stop happening

item.title: Vernacular Autoethnosis
item.description: Vernacular autoethnosis is (definition:: the practicing|practice of self-determining an ethos through creating and using chosen ritual|rituals, story|stories, [language pattern|language patterns](language pattern|language patterns), and [word choice|word choices](word choice|word choices)).

item.title: Virtual Environment
item.description: An environment constructed with [digital computer|digital computers](digital computer|digital computers) or through imagination.

item.title: Waste Managing
item.description: Waste managing is (definition:: the managing of waste, such as sewage).

item.title: Sitemap
item.description:
A sitemap is a file, usually in XML format, that lists the pages of a website along with metadata about each page (last modification date, update frequency, relative priority). Search engines read sitemaps to discover and index content more efficiently, especially for large sites or pages that are not well-linked from the rest of the site.

The sitemap protocol was jointly adopted by Google, Yahoo, and Microsoft in 2006. A site’s robots.txt file typically includes a reference to the sitemap location so that crawlers can find it automatically.

item.title: W3C
item.description:
The World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) is the main international standards organization for the Web. Founded by Tim Berners-Lee in 1994, the W3C develops specifications and guidelines intended to ensure the long-term growth and interoperability of web technologies.

Key W3C specifications include HTML, CSS, the Web Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), and the ActivityPub protocol for federated social networking. The W3C also maintains specifications for RDF and JSON-LD, which provide structured data vocabularies for the semantic web.

item.title: Recuperation
item.description:
Recuperation is the process by which radical ideas, practices, and aesthetics are absorbed into dominant culture, stripped of their oppositional content, and repurposed to serve the systems they originally challenged. The term comes from the Situationist International (Guy Debord, Raoul Vaneigem), who argued that capitalism has the capacity to turn any critique into a commodity — including the critique of commodification itself.

The mechanism is not suppression but incorporation. Rebellion becomes fashion. Resistance becomes a brand. Revolutionary slogans become advertising copy. The content of the critique is emptied out; the form is preserved and sold back. This process does not require conspiracy — it follows from the logic of a system that can absorb anything that can be marketed, and the market’s indifference to the meaning of what it circulates.

item.title: Tishoumaren
item.description:
Tishoumaren (also called assouf) is a musical style developed by Tuareg musicians in the Saharan and Sahelian regions of Niger, Mali, Algeria, and Libya. The term means roughly “nostalgia” or “longing” in Tamasheq, reflecting the music’s origins in the displacement and dispossession of Tuareg communities.

The style emerged in the 1970s and 1980s among Tuareg youth in exile and refugee camps, particularly those displaced by post-independence state repression in Mali and Niger. Musicians adapted electric guitar techniques to Tuareg melodic and rhythmic patterns, creating a distinctive sound built on pentatonic scales, interlocking rhythms, and extended improvisations. The guitar replaced the traditional tehardent (a three-stringed lute) while maintaining its melodic vocabulary.

item.title: agda
item.description:
Agda is a dependently typed functional programming language and proof assistant based on Martin-Löf type theory. Programs and proofs are the same thing: a proof of a proposition is a well-typed program whose type is that proposition. Agda’s type checker verifies totality (termination and coverage), ensuring that every proof is valid.

Key ideas

  • Dependent types: types can depend on values, enabling precise specifications and theorem statements within the type system.
  • Proofs as programs: there is no separate tactic language. Proofs are written as function definitions using pattern matching and equational reasoning.
  • Constructive by default: Agda is natively intuitionistic. Excluded middle is not assumed; classical axioms can be postulated when needed.
  • Totality checking: all functions must terminate and cover all cases. This prevents inconsistency.

Uses

  • Formalizing mathematical structures and verifying their properties.
  • Teaching type theory and constructive logic through executable proofs.
  • Prototyping dependently typed programs where correctness is enforced by the type system.

See also

  • Lean (proof assistant with tactic framework and Mathlib library); Coq, Isabelle/HOL (other proof assistants).

item.title: covid-19
item.description:
COVID-19 (Coronavirus Disease 2019) is the respiratory illness caused by the virus SARS-CoV-2. The World Health Organization declared it a pandemic in March 2020. The disease spread worldwide, killing millions, and prompted lockdowns, border closures, and mass vaccination campaigns across most countries.

Symptoms range from mild (cough, fever, fatigue, loss of taste or smell) to severe (pneumonia, organ failure, death). Severity correlates with age, pre-existing conditions, and access to medical care – a distribution that tracks existing social inequalities rather than cutting across them.

item.title: mathematical universe
item.description:
A mathematical universe is a formal structure within which mathematical objects, relations, and operations are defined: the domain of discourse for a given mathematical system. It specifies what exists (which objects), how those objects relate, and what operations can be performed on them.

The concept plays a role analogous to what set theory plays for standard mathematics — it provides the foundational context that gives meaning to all subsequent statements. A mathematical statement is not true or false in isolation but true or false within a given mathematical universe.

item.title: static site generator
item.description:
A static site generator (SSG) is software that takes source files – typically written in Markdown or a similar lightweight markup language – and produces plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files that can be served directly by a web server without any server-side processing at request time.

This site (emsenn.net) is built with quartz|Quartz, a static site generator designed for publishing Obsidian vaults as websites. Other well-known SSGs include Hugo, Jekyll, Eleventy, and Astro.

item.title: lean
item.description:
Lean is an interactive theorem prover and functional programming language with a small trusted kernel. Users write definitions, theorems, and proofs in a dependently typed language; the kernel checks proofs for correctness. Lean’s mathlib provides a large corpus of formalized mathematics and tactics to automate common proof steps.

Key ideas

  • Dependent types: types can depend on values, enabling precise specifications.
  • Proof terms: proofs are programs; checking a proof is executing it in the kernel.
  • Tactics: scripted proof steps that build proof terms interactively.

Uses

  • Certifying properties of algorithms, protocols, or models.
  • Teaching formal reasoning with immediate proof checking.
  • Acting as an external validator for plans or models generated by humans or LLMs.

See also

  • Coq, Isabelle/HOL (other proof assistants); mathlib (Lean’s main library).

item.title: petri net
item.description:
A Petri net is a bipartite graph of places (which hold tokens) and transitions (which consume/produce tokens). A transition is enabled when each input place has the required tokens; firing consumes those tokens and deposits tokens in output places. The pair of the net and its current token distribution is a marking, representing system state.

Key properties

  • Reachability: whether a marking (e.g., “done”) can be achieved from the start.
  • Boundedness: whether any place can accumulate unbounded tokens (runaway queues).
  • Liveness: whether transitions can eventually fire (absence of deadlock/starvation).
  • Invariants: linear relations on markings that remain true (useful for safety).

Uses

  • Modeling concurrent workflows, resource flow, and synchronization.
  • Checking if a process can complete without deadlock or overflow.
  • Serving as an executable specification for agent/skill pipelines.

See also

  • Colored Petri nets (typed tokens), workflow nets (process soundness).

item.title: groundhog autonomous zone
item.description:
The Groundhog Autonomous Zone ("GAZ") is an unincorporated association operating under these directives ("GAZ Directives").

The GAZ centers Black liberation and Indigenous sovereignty ("purpose of the GAZ", "GAZ's purpose").

The GAZ does de∥antide\|anticolonial land stewardship.

The GAZ educates about colonialsm and how to act against it.

The GAZ provides ways of living that are not supportive of colonialism.

The GAZ holds these things and the acts and artifacts they cause and create as having value ("GAZ Values").

People are identified with one or more labels based on their relationship with the GAZ and/or colonialism

Members of the GAZ are called Groundhogs.

  1. The Documentation Department maintains a list of members ("GAZ Membership List")

item.title: hospicing humanity
item.description:
Hospicing humanity is the recognition that humanity, as currently organized under capitalism and settler-colonial expansion, may be in a process of terminal decline — and that the appropriate response is to care for it through its dissolution.

The hospice metaphor is precise: hospice care does not aim at cure. It aims at dignity, reduced suffering, and honest presence with what is happening. Applied to humanity, this means the project of total liberation does not require believing that the current form of human civilization can be saved. It may involve helping it end with less suffering and more grace.

item.title: Norway
item.description:
Norway (Norwegian: Norge or Noreg) is a Nordic country on the Scandinavian Peninsula in Northern Europe. It shares borders with Sweden, Finland, and Russia, and has an extensive Atlantic and Arctic coastline shaped by fjords.

Norway is notable in the context of this vault for its approach to resource governance and social democracy, and as a reference point in discussions of energy transition, sovereign wealth management, and Indigenous rights (the Sámi people, whose traditional territory spans northern Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Russia). Its sovereign wealth fund, the Government Pension Fund Global, is frequently cited in discussions of how resource extraction revenue can be managed across generations.

item.title: observing
item.description:
Observing is the act of entering into a relation with a thing in such a way that a quality of the thing becomes stable and communicable.

In conventional epistemology, observing is a neutral act: the observer is transparent, and what is observed exists independently of the observation. In the relational framework, this account needs revision. Observing is relational: the quality observed emerges from the relation between the thing and the observer. Different modes of observation — different relational stances — produce different qualities.

item.title: total liberation
item.description:
Total liberation is the political position that the liberation of humans, non-human animals, and the Earth are facets of the same analysis. Domination does not apply only to one category of being; systems of control over land, animals, and human bodies share common structures and common beneficiaries.

The term emerges from the convergence of anarchism, animal liberation, and earth liberation movements. Where single-issue politics addresses one dimension of domination at a time, total liberation argues that this fragmentation mirrors and reinforces the systems being challenged. A project of human liberation that leaves animal agriculture and ecological extraction untouched has not reached its roots.

item.title: brave old world
item.description:
Brave Old World is an open-source role-playing game (RPG) where 2 or more players create a story by having a structured conversation. It is a traditional RPG played with a couple of dice and your imagination.

Brave Old World is a game about asking questions. Who is your adventurer? Why are they with the party? Where is the dragon’s secret lair? Is the mayor’s daughter really planning to burn down the town mill?

item.title: brave old world podcast
item.description:
The Brave Old World Podcast was an actual-play Dungeons & Dragons show where emsenn hosted and ran a campaign set in the Brave Old World setting.

Original Copy

Brave Old World is a make-believe world friends explored together with emsenn as the narrator. Sessions were streamed live on Twitch and later released as podcast episodes, aiming for the feel of an old-time radio play driven by dice and shared imagination.
